front: Partita: set of three, paired with three sonatas for solo violin by Bach; the D-minor partita ends with a massive Chaconne; revived by Joseph Joachim
back: Sonatas and Partitas for Solo Violin (Bach)
work: Sonatas and Partitas for Solo Violin (BWV 1001–1006)

front: Partita: probable inspiration was Johann Paul von Westhoff's six solo violin partitas, which made extensive use of double stops
back: Sonatas and Partitas for Solo Violin (Bach)
work: Sonatas and Partitas for Solo Violin (BWV 1001–1006)

front: Partita: Chaconne is the fifth movement of the D-minor partita; 64 variations on a 4-bar romanesca ostinato; called 'one of the greatest achievements of any man' by Joshua Bell
back: Partita No. 2 in D Minor – Chaconne (Bach)
work: Partita No. 2 in D Minor – Chaconne (BWV 1004)

front: Partita: seven-movement E major partita opens with a 'Preludio' (later arranged as Sinfonia for Cantata 29) and includes a 'Gavotte en Rondeau'
Partita: B-minor violin partita; each of the four movements paired with a 'Double'; ends with a Tempo di Borea instead of the expected Gigue
Partita: six keyboard partitas form Clavier-Übung I; tonics follow expanding intervals in alternating directions: B-flat, C, A, D, G, E
Partita: Bach's only work for solo (unaccompanied) flute
back: Partita for Solo Flute in A Minor (Bach)
work: Partita for Solo Flute in A Minor (BWV 1013)

front: Partita: Caroline Shaw's 2013 work for 8 voices; begins 'To the side'; includes 'the detail of the pattern is movement'; uses Sacred Harp style
